device_name: EFAI Bonesuite XR Bone Age Pro Assessment System (BAP-XR-100)
intended_use: EFAI BONESUITE XR BONE AGE PRO ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (EFAI BAPXR) is designed to view and quantify bone age from 2D Posterior Anterior (PA) view of left-hand radiographs using deep learning techniques to
intended_use_full: EFAI BONESUITE XR BONE AGE PRO ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (EFAI BAPXR) is designed to view and quantify bone age from 2D Posterior Anterior (PA) view of left-hand radiographs using deep learning techniques to aid in the analysis of bone age assessment of patients between 2 to 16 years old for pediatric radiologists. The results should not be relied upon alone by pediatric radiologists to make diagnostic decisions. The images shall be with left hand and wrist fully visible within the field of view, and shall be without any major bone destruction, deformity, fracture, excessive motion, or other major artifacts.
ground_truth: Average of bone age assessment independently done by four U.S. board-certified expert radiologists following Greulich-Pyle Atlas with multiple checkpoints to ensure consistency and consensus
other_metrics: Deming regression intercept: -0.07 (95% CI: [-0.13, -0.01]); slope: 1.00 (95% CI: [0.99, 1.00]); 88% of cases with difference less than 0.5 years between ground truth and EFAI BAPXR output; Bland-Altman 95% limits of agreement: -0.517 to 0.743 years
